title: Kokkamangalam St. Thomas Church
tagline: Kokkamangalam St. Thomas Church — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Kokkamangalam St. Thomas Church is a much-loved spiritual town in Kerala, known for temples and pilgrimage.
overview.description: St. Thomas Syro-Malabar Catholic Church, Kokkamangalam, popularly known as Kokkamangalam Church, which holds a midway position among the seven churches founded by St. Thomas, is in the Syro-Malabar Catholic Archdiocese of Ernakulam-Angamaly, in the South Indian state of Kerala. St.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kokkamangalam
topPlaces.description: Kokkamangalam also known as Gokkamangalam is a village in Alappuzha district of Kerala state, south India. It is situated in between Cochin and Kumarakom and on the western shore of Vembanad Lake, 5 km east of the town of Cherthala, which has the nearest railway station.

topPlaces.name: Kokkothamangalam
topPlaces.description: Kokkothamangalam is a census town in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Peruviruthy
topPlaces.description: Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple is a temple dedicated to Duryodhana, is located in Poruvazhy Village of Kunnathur Tehsil in the Kollam district of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Thanneermukkom Bund
topPlaces.description: The Thanneermukkom Bund (Thannermukkom Salt Water Barrier) was constructed as a part of the Kuttanad Development Scheme to prevent tidal action and intrusion of salt water into the Kuttanad low-lands across Vembanad Lake between Thannermukkom on west and Vechur on east. Thanneermukkom Bund was constructed in 1974 and is functional since 1976.

topPlaces.name: Chemmanathukara
topPlaces.description: Chemmanathukara is a place in Vaikom Village, Kottayam District, Kerala, India. It is part of T.V. Puram Panchayat (Thirumani Venkitapuram Panchayat).

topPlaces.name: Karthyayani Devi Temple, Cherthala
topPlaces.description: Karthyayani Devi Temple, Cherthala is a famous Hindu temple located at Cherthala. Iratti and Thadi are the famous vazhipadu, Cherthala pooram is the second famous pooram in Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Cherthala
topPlaces.description: Cherthala is a suburb and satellite town of Kochi City located in the Alappuzha district of Kerala, India, along National Highway 66. As per the 2011 Indian census, Cherthala has a population of 45,821 people, and a population density of 2,727/km2 (7,060/sq mi).

topPlaces.name: Vechoor
topPlaces.description: Vechoor is a village in Vaikom taluk, Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India. Vechoor is a short drive away from one of Kerala's tourist destinations, Kumarakom.

items.note: Kokkamangalam also known as Gokkamangalam is a village in Alappuzha district of Kerala state, south India. It is situated in between Cochin and Kumarakom and on the western shore of Vembanad Lake, 5 km east of the town of Cherthala, which has the nearest railway station.
Kokkothamangalam is a census town in Alappuzha district in the Indian state of Kerala.
Poruvazhy Peruviruthy Malanada Temple is a temple dedicated to Duryodhana, is located in Poruvazhy Village of Kunnathur Tehsil in the Kollam district of Kerala, India.

The Thanneermukkom Bund (Thannermukkom Salt Water Barrier) was constructed as a part of the Kuttanad Development Scheme to prevent tidal action and intrusion of salt water into the Kuttanad low-lands across Vembanad Lake between Thannermukkom on west and Vechur on east. Thanneermukkom Bund was constructed in 1974 and is functional since 1976.
Chemmanathukara is a place in Vaikom Village, Kottayam District, Kerala, India. It is part of T.V. Puram Panchayat (Thirumani Venkitapuram Panchayat).
Karthyayani Devi Temple, Cherthala is a famous Hindu temple located at Cherthala. Iratti and Thadi are the famous vazhipadu, Cherthala pooram is the second famous pooram in Kerala.

Cherthala is a suburb and satellite town of Kochi City located in the Alappuzha district of Kerala, India, along National Highway 66. As per the 2011 Indian census, Cherthala has a population of 45,821 people, and a population density of 2,727/km2 (7,060/sq mi).
Vechoor is a village in Vaikom taluk, Kottayam district in the state of Kerala, India. Vechoor is a short drive away from one of Kerala's tourist destinations, Kumarakom.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kokkamangalam St. Thomas Church?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kokkamangalam St. Thomas Church is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kokkamangalam St. Thomas Church?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (52 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (33 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kokkamangalam St. Thomas Church?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kokkamangalam St. Thomas Church at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kokkamangalam St. Thomas Church?
faq.a: Stays in Kokkamangalam St. Thomas Church start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kokkamangalam St. Thomas Church?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kokkamangalam, Kokkothamangalam, Peruviruthy, Thanneermukkom Bund, Chemmanathukara.
